records.ety: [L. praescriptivus of a demurrer or legal exception.]

defs: Consisting in, or acquired by, immemorial or long-continued use and enjoyment; as, a prescriptive right of title; pleading the continuance and authority of long custom.
quotes: The right to be drowsy in protracted toil has become prescriptive.

defs: Of or pertaining to the doctrine that acceptable grammatical rules should be prescribed by authority, rather than be determined by common usage.
